what_it_does: An acceptance test that exercises the MCP server's cognition tool functions by calling them directly, verifying agent-facing inspect/configure/run/embed/inspect/create/map/reduce behavior and asserting that autonomous build-dispatch mutations stay operator-gated.
observations: Requires COMPANY_STORE to be set before importing the MCP server because the server binds a module-level SUITE at import time.
Probes resident brain and embedder services via HTTP /models health checks before deciding which live checks to run.
Drives the underlying MCP tool functions directly rather than going over the MCP transport.
GPU-gates the embed path: if the embedder is not resident it proves the code path with ensure=False and expects a loud failure, never a blind eviction of the live brain.
Tests inspect_address round-trip and fail-loud behavior on unresolvable addresses.
Tests that propose_role surfaces a kept inbox item but does not write a roles/ file.
Asserts the consolidated create(kind=) tool is registered and the eight flat create_* tools are removed.
Tests run_items fan-out and run_reduce deterministic rule join plus role-based synthesis.
Enforces the operator-only floor by AST-scanning the server source for forbidden calls and subprocess/implement imports.
standouts: VERIFY BY USE: it drives real MCP tool functions rather than reading code.
GPU-gated embed design that prefers a loud code-path proof over evicting a resident model.
AST-based runtime floor scan forbidding resolve_surfaced, dispatch_decision, apply_role_delete, and claude -p launch paths.
Consolidation of eight flat create tools into a single create(kind=) tool.
conventions: Uses global PASS/ok counters and a simple check(label, cond) helper.
Heavy inline comments and docstring reference tickets (#53, #58, C9.2) and design rationale.
Mixes runtime behavior tests with static source-code AST assertions.
Uses try/except blocks to assert fail-loud behavior.
Relies on module-level import side effects and requires environment setup before import.
concerns: Global state mutation and sys.path manipulation.
AST floor scan is brittle to refactoring, renaming, or code moved into nested modules.
Live checks depend on external services and are skipped rather than exercised with fixtures or mocks.
Broad except Exception catches may hide unexpected failures.
Graph node creation silently swallows exceptions.
notes: A runnable acceptance harness that proves agents can use the MCP cognition face end-to-end while keeping autonomous build-dispatch decisions operator-gated.
questions: What is the exact shape and API of srv.SUITE and its registries?
How are the eight flat create tools removed and the consolidated create tool registered in mcp_face/tools/create.py?
What value does fabric config DEFAULT_EMBED_DIM resolve to?
How are resolve_surfaced and dispatch_decision implemented in the wire modules?
What is the relationship between this file and tests/direct_create_acceptance.py referenced in the comments?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to verify by use that the MCP engine exposure works and the operator-only floor holds; the file matches that purpose and additionally covers the #58 direct-create consolidation.
apparent_themes: Verify by use, not by inspection.
Reuse existing Suite/engine paths; no parallel engine.
Registry-is-truth: roles/rules declare ops, no fabrication.
Tool consolidation for a cleaner MCP surface.
intention_signals: It is trying to prove that the MCP server exposes cognition capabilities safely for agents, that all headline operations work end-to-end, and that the new agent face cannot autonomously trigger build or dispatch decisions.
novelty: It combines live-service acceptance testing with a static AST scan of the server module to enforce a governance floor, and it explicitly GPU-gates embedding to avoid evicting a resident model.
contribution: A runnable acceptance harness that documents and validates the MCP cognition surface, including the consolidated create tool, GPU-gated embed path, inspect_address round-trip, run_items/run_reduce orchestration, and the operator-only build-dispatch floor.
summary_for_embedding: This file is an acceptance test that drives the MCP server's cognition tools to verify agent-facing generate, embed, inspect, propose, create, map, and reduce operations while asserting that autonomous build-dispatch mutations remain operator-gated.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to prove by actual use that the cognition engine is reachable through the MCP face under the same Suite/engine paths as the human API and that the operator-only floor holds on that face.

symbols.does: Increments PASS if cond is truthy, otherwise marks ok False and prints a failure line.
symbols.description: A lightweight assertion helper used throughout the test; it maintains global PASS/ok counters and prints ok/FAIL lines for each labeled check.

symbols.does: Probes a resident service by requesting /models and checks for a non-empty data list.
symbols.description: A small health-check helper that returns True only if the given base URL responds with HTTP 200 and a populated models list, used to decide whether live brain and embedder checks can run.
